name: Swim Day 3: Pull & Chill
description: Jess: Upper body focus with pull buoy — let the legs rest after kick day. ~30 min, 1200-1500y. Smooth pulls, sculling, backstroke. Chill vibes.

exercises.notes: Easy backstroke to start — face out of the water, find your rhythm.
Buoy between thighs. Long, smooth pulls. Focus on the catch — fingertips down, elbow high. Rest :15 between.
Pull buoy backstroke — so floaty. Steady rotation, pinky-first entry.
Scull on your back: head-first, feet-first, mid-scull, dealer's choice. Small figure-8s with the hands.
Catch-up to reinforce that long front-end feeling.
Easy breast — long glides, open hips.

tips: Pull buoy takes pressure off the legs — enjoy the float
The buoy lifts your hips which feels amazing with a growing belly
Sculling is meditation in the pool — let yourself zone out
If you only have 20 min, skip the sculling block and just do the pull set
